results.title: Mid • Machine Learning Engineer, Data Mining • Pittsburgh, PA | MindPal Jobs
highlights:
# Machine Learning Engineer, Data Mining
...
Mission Summary:At Motional, we're transforming how autonomous vehicles discover critical intelligence hidden within petabytes of multimodal sensor data. Our next-generation autonomous driving stack depends on finding the rare edge cases, long-tail scenarios, and model errors that matter most. Omnitag, our ML-powered multimodal data mining framework, is the engine that powers this discovery.As a Machine Learning Engineer on the Data Mining team, your mission is to help build the "Brain" of this engine. You will work with state-of-the-art foundation models to extract insights from Motional's driving data, working at the intersection of large-scale representation learning and data retrieval. By building smarter mining tools and efficient data pipelines, you will accelerate the model improvement lifecycle for teams working on post-training analysis, error diagnosis, and dataset curation.
...
- Build and Train ML Pipelines: Develop, train, and fine-tune machine learning models for multimodal sensor data (e.g., vision, LiDAR). Focus on implementing supervised and self-supervised learning approaches to improve data search and retrieval.
...
- Support Model Deployment: Implement scalable data preprocessing and augmentation pipelines. Assist in applying standard optimization techniques (e.g., batch inference, quantization) to ensure models run efficiently in production environments.
...
- Data Mining & Analysis: Help develop embedding-based search tools and "active learning" workflows to identify critical driving scenarios.
...
- BS or MS in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field.
- Hands-on experience with PyTorch (preferred) or TensorFlow/JAX. You should be comfortable training models and evaluating them using standard metrics.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python with the ability to write clean, modular, and well-documented code.
...
- Working knowledge of version control, unit testing, and basic software design patterns.
...
- Experience working with large datasets, including proficiency in SQL and data libraries like Pandas and NumPy.
...
- MS/PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field.
- Experience with agentic systems, autonomous reasoning, chain-of-thought models, or LLM-based planning.
- Background in autonomous driving, robotics, or real-time decision-making systems.
...
- Familiarity with multimodal learning, sensor fusion, or embodied AI.
...
- Experience building active learning loops, using the model to find the data that breaks the model.
...
- Experience with ML-based data mining, active learning, or contrastive learning.
...
- Knowledge of model serving tools (TF Serving, Triton, TorchServe) and MLOps platforms.
...
- Publication in top-tier conferences (e.g., ICCV, CVPR, ECCV)
...
Formed as a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and Aptiv, Motional is fundamentally changing how people move through their lives. Headquartered in Boston, Motional has operations in the U.S and Asia. For more information, visit www.Motional.com and follow us on Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube.
...
Motional is a driverless technology company operating in the autonomous vehicle industry, focused on making self-driving transportation safe, reliable, and accessible. Formed as a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and Aptiv, the company combines automotive manufacturing expertise with advanced mobility technology. Headquartered in Boston with operations across the United States and Asia, Motional is scaling toward commercialization of its autonomous systems and has achieved major industry milestones, including launching a public robotaxi pilot and operating one of the longest-running public robotaxi fleets. The company is driven by a people-first mission to create safer roadways and more equitable transportation, emphasizing innovation, diversity, inclusion, and a collaborative global culture to transform how people move within their communities.

highlights:
# Staff Machine Learning Engineer
...
As a Staff Machine Learning Engineer, you will serve as a technical leader defining the roadmap and architecture for the machine learning systems that power our data discovery and model improvement lifecycles. Rather than focusing on a single specialized domain, you will leverage your broad ML expertise to architect massive, scalable systems, from multimodal representation learning and active learning loops to hyper-efficient production inference. You will own system-level architecture, lead multi-quarter, multi-person initiatives, and partner across the engineering organization to unblock teams and influence our department-wide technical strategy. By establishing robust processes and mentoring those around you, you will ensure our ML platforms act as a reliable, mission-critical engine for the entire autonomy stack.

highlights:
Aptiv is a global industrial technology company positioning itself as a leader in intelligent edge computing, developing AI-powered solutions across automotive, robotics, aerospace, and other mission-critical applications [1]. At CES 2026, the company showcased its expanded technology portfolio, demonstrating how automotive AI expertise can power applications beyond vehicles [1]. Aptiv's strategy centers on enabling devices to sense, think, and act in real time through edge processing rather than cloud dependency, with proven technologies from millions of vehicles now extending to collaborative robots and aerospace platforms [2]. The company has strategic partnerships with Robust.AI and Vecna Robotics for AI-powered robotics development [3][4], and is preparing to separate its Electrical Distribution Systems business to create two focused companies [5].

my achievement as

Agentic AI

025 in Bangalore!

skill for creating
...
the future of
...
Spent a thought-provoking day at Columbia University’s Data Science and AI Exchange (DAX 2026). While the tech was impressive, the shift in how we approach AI systems was the real takeaway.
...
🛡️ The Shift in Agentic Safety: We often talk about "human-in-the-loop," but the panel on Agentic AI suggested a more scalable model: Human-as-Architect. Instead of manually auditing every AI output (the ultimate bottleneck), our role is shifting toward engineering the pre-conditions —the guardrails and behavioral constraints—within which an agent operates.
...
⚖️ The Explainability Paradox: I loved the provocation that while we demand perfect transparency from AI, humans remain the "ultimate black box." We are holding algorithms to a standard of logic that we rarely meet ourselves. The challenge for our generation of data scientists isn't just making models explainable, but making them accountable in ways humans often aren't.

The 6 layers of #GenAI tech stack every AI/ML engineer should know.
...
The GenAI technology stack is a sophisticated, layered architecture designed to harness the power of large language models (LLMs) for real-world applications. 

At its foundation lies the Infrastructure layer, comprising cloud platforms like AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure, or on-premise servers. This layer provides the essential computational resources and scalability needed to support AI operations.
...
Built upon this is the Data layer, housing databases, data lakes, vector stores, and knowledge graphs. This layer is crucial for managing the vast amounts of data required for training and operating AI systems
...
and updating of
...
The LLM layer sits at the core of the stack, featuring powerful models such as GPT-4, Claude, and BERT. These models serve as the engines of natural language understanding and generation, capable of processing and producing human-like text across various domains.
...
The API layer acts as a bridge, exposing the capabilities of these LLMs through standardized interfaces like the OpenAI API or Anthropic API. This abstraction allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into their applications without dealing with the complexities of model deployment and management.
...
The Application layer leverages frameworks like Langchain and Semantic Kernel to build sophisticated AI applications. These tools provide high-level abstractions for common AI tasks, enabling rapid development of complex, context-aware applications.
...

results.title: Rahul G.
highlights:
Senior Software Engineer | AD
...
Virtual Simulation & Multi-Sensor Modeling | AI-Augmented Engineering (RAG/Agentic AI) | Lead for High-Scale Linux/HPCC Infrastructure | 2026 Focus: NVIDIA Cosmos & AWS SOTIF Scale-out
...
Engineer ADAS Integration, Simulation &
...
Architecture at [Aptiv
...
aptiv)
...
I am a Senior Software Engineer and Systems Architect specializing in the "closed-loop" intersection of ADAS algorithms, multi-sensor modeling, and high-scale virtual simulation. With a career spanning Aptiv, Ford, and Caterpillar, I bridge the gap between low-level embedded hardware and massive, cloud-scale software-defined vehicle (SDV) validation. From Embedded Roots to System Design My foundation is built on deep-tier embedded systems—mastering C/C++, RTOS (FreeRTOS), and communication protocols (CAN, LIN, Ethernet, UDP). I have evolved this expertise into complex System Design, architecting modular frameworks like the Northstar ACF that integrate diverse sensor stacks (Radar SM2, Vision, USS) into unified, reusable simulation libraries. My Core Philosophy: Root-Cause Engineering I am known as a "go-to" engineer for complex integrations and deep-system debugging. Whether it is navigating ME protocol migrations, managing DLL separation, or stabilizing multi-executable data sharing, I focus on delivering robust, architectural fixes—not just patches—that ensure long-term platform stability. At Aptiv (Present): Currently, I lead the technical enablement of ADAS simulation at scale. I’ve spearheaded the transition to Linux/HPCC-ready Docker execution and Bazel-based build systems, unlocking native parallel simulation that has significantly reduced "time-to-first-signal" for our global R&D teams. AI-Augmented Engineering: I don’t just build ADAS; I leverage Agentic AI and RAG pipelines to accelerate the engineering lifecycle. I co-created an internal RAG assistant for ADAS Regulations, moving beyond manual searches to LLM-driven knowledge retrieval for 1,000+ users. Looking Ahead (2026): My focus is on the next frontier of SOTIF: NVIDIA Cosmos for photorealistic multimodal validation and AWS multi-container deployments for massive scale-out. Technical Pillars: • System Design & Architecture: Modular Framework Design, Host-SW Integration, IPC (UDP/Shared Memory), Architecture Reviews. • ADAS & Simulation: MIL/SIL/HIL Validation, OSI/FMUs, CarMaker, Scenario Automation, Vehicle Dynamics. • Embedded Systems: C/C++, RTOS, Device Drivers, CAN/LIN/Ethernet, ECU Applications, Low-level Debugging (Lauterbach/Trace32). • Infrastructure & Scale: Linux Enablement, Docker/Bazel, HPCC Scale, AWS Cloud Containers. • AI/ML: RAG Pipelines, GenAI for Automation, CNNs for Diagnostic Tooling
...
Core member of the Global Simulation & Tools organization. I architect reusable ADAS simulation frameworks and high-scale validation pipelines, bridging the gap between algorithm development (Algo/SWE.6) and massive-scale HPCC/Cloud execution. Key Impact & Technical Leadership:
...
- AI Innovation (2025/2026): Co-created an internal RAG-based ADAS Regulations Search assistant; scaled to 1,000+ R&D users to automate technical knowledge retrieval. Currently leading investigations into NVIDIA Cosmos for photorealistic multimodal validation and AWS SOTIF scale-out.
...

Lead AI/ML solutions for General Motors' Product Safety Data Analytics team, developing end-to-end machine learning pipelines including generative AI and agentic AI systems in a hybrid role at the Warren, Michigan technical center.
...
## Job description
...
Our Product Safety Data Analytics team is seeking an experienced AI/ML Data Scientist with hands-on experience in the full end to end data science lifecycle for artificial intelligence & machine learning.
...
This role requires programming and statistical techniques to solve complex problems and work within a team framework on new AI/ML solutions.
...
As an AI/ML Data Scientist, you will be responsible for working with our customers to understand their challenges and needs, develop new machine learning solutions, work with ML Ops in a cloud environment, develop proof of concepts for new generative AI solutions and Agentic AI solutions.
**What You’ll Do**
...
* Work with our existing business partners to identify opportunities for new AI/ML solutions to enhance processes and enable our team members to be more efficient
...
* Train new machine learning models to solve complex business problems.
...
* Prototype new AI solutions, including Generative AI, to solve business problems.
...
* Develop new Agentic AI solutions to streamline business process
...
* Ehnance existing model solutions as business and technology continues to evolve
...
* Provide guidance on business problems using statistical methods and can craft ad-hoc reports to share findings and recommendations with business partners.
...
* Build statistical models that depict company-wide trends.
...
* Perform testing and validation of data sets.
...
**Your Skills & Abilities (Required Qualifications)**
...
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
...
* 5+ years of experience in machine learning, engineering, data science, or a related field
...
* Looking for experience in the following:
...
* **Programming & Frameworks**: Python, R, Java, PySpark, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, LangChain, SQL
...
* **Machine Learning & AI**: Large Language Models (LLMs), Generative AI, RAG, Generative AI, Deep learning, Reinforcement Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), SVM, XGBoost, Random Forest, Decision Trees, Clustering
...
* **Data Engineering**: Databricks, Hadoop, SQL, Data Pipelines, Data Preprocessing & Feature Engineering
...
* **Cloud & Big Data Platforms: (**Preferred Microsoft Azure (Data Lake, Machine Learning, Databricks), **Nice to Have** (AWS (S3, SageMaker, Bedrock) or Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, Dataflow, AI Platform) )
...
* **Deployment & MLOps:** **MLflow**, Model Monitoring & Versioning, Docker & Kubernetes, **GitHub**, Jira
...
* **Data Analysis & Visualization**: Tableau, PowerBI, Pandas, NumPy
...
* Proven track record delivering AI/ML solutions to customers that get continued usage and drive value
...
**What Will Give You A Competitive Edge (Preferred qualifications)**
...
* Deep knowledge of GM’s Data Ecosystem
...
* Deep knowledge of GM’s Cloud Technology Stack for Data Science
...
* Deep knowledge of Global Product Safety’s SFI Process
...
